Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and brown, cloudy urine. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, and possible liver involvement. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria. Recommend further evaluation for renal and hepatic pathology, and review for possible pre-analytical or analytical errors regarding HbA1c. Clinical correlation is advised.
